title: Tetrapilus gagnepainii
taxonomy: Tetrapilus gagnepainii belongs to the family Rutaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus or rue family. It is a species of flowering plant that is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Vietnam, Laos, and Thailand. The classification of Tetrapilus gagnepainii is further divided into the genus Tetrapilus and the species T. gagnepainii. Within the Rutaceae family, Tetrapilus gagnepainii is closely related to other plants like citrus and orange trees. The exact classification of Tetrapilus gagnepainii may vary depending on the source and the latest botanical research.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular protective effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of hypertension, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions.
